Document:
# Dropships and Aircraft
These regulations govern the authority, modification and operational use of all aircraft and dropships.
## Flight Crew Authority - Flight Crew may modify their aircraft with permission from a member of the command, as long as the modifications provide a benefit in mind, not solely for decorative purposes. - Commander or Adjutant may deny any modifications made to the aircraft. - Flight crew may deny access to personnel onboard their ship unless said personnel are deploying to the area of operations or returning from them. - Flight Crew may remove any personnel, except Command Staff, for any reason unless doing so would breach the previous ruling.
## Aircraft Integrity - Dismantling aircraft components to obtain materials for other purposes is strictly forbidden. - Ship doors must remain intact at all times. - Ship doors may not be tampered with in a way that causes them to remain INOP in flight (i.e. bolting them open then taking off.)
## Seating and Cargo - Seating capacity may be reduced to accommodate additional ammunition or cargo. - Seating may not be completely removed on the primary dropship; seating may be completely removed on the Gunship if it is NOT the only operable ship.
## Primary Transport Role - If a dropship becomes inaccessible or inoperable, another aircraft must assume the transport role. - The replacement aircraft must be configured with appropriate troop seating whenever possible.
## Parachute Operations - When conducting parachute drops, the Flight Crew are responsible for: - Ensuring the drop zone is safe. - Notifying personnel when to deploy. - Blind Drops are permitted with groups of four or fewer members and with permission from a member of the command staff. - Blind Drops with five or more members require a scout first, then all remaining personnel drop at their location via a flare or laser-designated mark, also requiring command staff permission.
